Prompt
U
Oracle Events
1. Search CRM for Mosaic deals using crm_search_deals (dealname: "Mosaic"). Find: deal_mosaic_okoye_apr2026_risk - damage claim for Zara Okoye relocation, prototype Unit #1 ($90K) with cracked servo motor housing and 4+ degree calibration drift, $50K vendor liability cap, $40K uncovered gap, Blessing Okafor signed off remotely from Philadelphia, delivery signed by intern Kyle Britten; deal_mosaic_hartley_apr2026 - Deshawn Hartley Tampa→Denver, $1,520, hard deadline April 19 for Automate 2026 trade show, vendor Swift Relocations, coordinator Suki Patel; deal_mosaic_q2 - Andre Washington Columbus GA→Seattle WA, $4,500, closedwon.
2. Search CRM engagements for Mosaic history using crm_list_engagements (company_ids: ["company_mosaic"]). Find: engagement_mosaic_damage_incident_041526 with full chain-of-custody timeline (Heartland crew April 14 7:30 AM, Blessing's remote digital signature ~9:45 AM, loading completed ~10:00 AM, overnight stop unconfirmed, delivery April 15 ~9:15 AM signed by intern Kyle Britten); engagement_mosaic_apr2026_relocation_wave showing April overview with 3 new relocations (Okoye $2,700, Hartley $1,520, Tanaka $2,770) and 6 total active - lists Andre Washington as Detroit→Atlanta (completed), does NOT include his Columbus→Seattle relocation.
3. Search Airtable for Deshawn Hartley using airtable_search_records (field_name: "Name", value: "Deshawn Hartley"). Find: recDeshawnHartleyTampaDenver - Tampa→Denver, Status: In Progress, dates April 14–19, coordinator Suki Patel, hard deadline for Automate 2026 trade show, standard household goods, furnished 1BR near downtown Denver preferred.
4. Search Airtable for Andre Washington using airtable_search_records (field_name: "Name", value: "Andre Washington"). Find: recReloc00000009 - Detroit MI→Atlanta GA, Status: Completed - no Airtable record exists for Columbus→Seattle. Stipend records: $1,200 standing desk (auto-approved in error, over $800 Mosaic cap), $350 coworking membership (auto-approved in error, coworking excluded from Mosaic policy), $89 ergonomic keyboard (duplicate receipt hash, status: Pending Approval).
5. Search QuickBooks for Mosaic billing using quickbooks_search_invoices and quickbooks_search_bills. Find: INV-2026-0411 ($9,010 for 3 April relocations - Okoye, Hartley, Tanaka), Invoice 1007 ($4,500 for Andre Washington Columbus GA→Seattle WA), CM-2026-0415 (credit memo, -$90,000 for prototype damage claim), bill_mosaic_damage_accrual_001 ($90,000 pending liability accrual, $50K vendor coverage cap, $40K uncovered exposure), bill_atlas_washington ($439.40 for flight UA 1842 CMH→SEA, Andre Washington, booking ref ATCMV-92847). QuickBooks confirms Columbus→Seattle is financially tracked despite being absent from Airtable and Linear.
6. Search emails using search_emails (query: "Tamara Byrd" or "prototype" or "damage" or "Andre Washington"). Find: Zara Okoye's damage report to Tamara (cracked servo motor, 4+ degree drift), Tamara's formal damage notification to Emeka requesting resolution within 48 hours, Emeka's response committing to 48-hour incident report, Emeka's complete incident report delivered to Tamara within the 48-hour window (full chain-of-custody, root cause, remediation plan), Emeka's Columbus→Seattle confirmation to Tamara (email_client_confirm_001, April 2 - flight/movers/apartment confirmed, DOB and personal contact still pending for coordinator briefing), Tamara's follow-up on Andre's relocation (April 10, asking for status update - read but unanswered), Andre Washington's pre-flight email to Emeka (April 13, asking about apartment keys, movers, coordinator briefing - read but unanswered), Tamara's earlier relocation requests for Deshawn Hartley and Keiko Tanaka.
7. Search Slack using conversations_search_messages (search_query: "Mosaic" or "damage" or "Washington"). Find: Emeka's high-severity escalation in #operations (C006), Chloe's chain-of-custody investigation (intern Kyle Britten signed delivery, not Zara), Blessing's admission of remote sign-off from Philadelphia, Hana's accounting entries (CM-2026-0415 credit memo and $90K liability accrual), Marcus's financial exposure analysis ($50K coverage cap, $40K gap), Emeka's Columbus→Seattle confirmation in #customer-engagement (April 2, "all wrapped up on our end" - notes still waiting on Andre's DOB and personal contact for coordinator briefing), Fatimah Al-Rashidi's flag in #operations (April 8, Andre's NDA/DOB still missing, blocking coordinator briefing, flight 6 days out).
8. Search Linear for Mosaic tickets using linear_list_issues (query: "Mosaic" or "Washington"). Find: #325 Apartment Search – Andre Washington – Atlanta (in_progress), #326 Flight Booking – DTW→ATL (todo), #327 Moving Coordination – Detroit→Atlanta (todo). All three are for the OLD Detroit→Atlanta move - no Linear tickets exist for Columbus→Seattle. Combined with Airtable showing Completed for the old move, this creates a full cross-system status discrepancy. Also finds DOT compliance tickets for Keiko Tanaka.
9. Look up contact emails using contacts_search_contacts (queries: "Tamara Byrd", "David Chen"). Find: tamara.byrd@mosaicrobotics.com, david.chen@moveops.com.
10. Log CRM engagement notes using crm_create_engagement on a Mosaic Robotics deal. Content: damage claim status ($90K prototype, $40K uncovered gap, Blessing's remote sign-off as root cause, incident report already delivered), Deshawn Hartley on track for April 19 hard deadline, Andre Washington cross-system discrepancy (Airtable/Linear show old Detroit→Atlanta, CRM deal and QuickBooks confirm Columbus→Seattle executed, unanswered emails from Tamara April 10 and Andre April 13, coordinator briefing never happened - NDA/DOB never collected per Fatimah's April 8 flag), stipend issues flagged.
11. Schedule call with Tamara using calendar_add_calendar_event (title: "Mosaic Account Review - Emeka & Tamara" or similar, attendees: emeka.diallo@moveops.com, tamara.byrd@mosaicrobotics.com, scheduled for April 17).
12. Send holding email to Tamara using send_email (sender: emeka.diallo@moveops.com, recipients: tamara.byrd@mosaicrobotics.com). Content acknowledges her concerns about the damage claim, Deshawn's Automate deadline, and Andre's move updates; promises detailed answers within 48 hours; mentions the scheduled April 17 call.
13. Brief David in #customer-engagement using conversations_add_message (channel_id: C002). Content flags Mosaic account needs leadership attention - $90K damage claim with $40K uncovered gap, Deshawn's April 19 hard deadline, Andre communication gap (unanswered emails, NDA/DOB never collected, coordinator briefing never executed, cross-system tracking failure), context that Mosaic is the newest client (~2 months) with damage exposure exceeding ~10x their total invoice revenue.

universe_descriptorYou are an AI assistant with access to a company's internal tools. Use the available tools to complete the entire task autonomously. No further user input will be given. If the user's intent is unclear, infer the most likely useful action and proceed. Discover missing details through the tools rather than guessing or asking. The current date is April 26, 2026. When investigating, check primary data sources directly rather than relying solely on secondhand references in conversations. Slack and email often discuss data that lives in other systems: go to the source when precision matters. About MoveOps Inc.: MoveOps is a corporate relocation management company. The following systems are used across the organization: - Email & Slack: Day-to-day communication, client correspondence, team coordination - Airtable: Relocation records, move tracking, stipend/expense records, vendor assignments - CRM (HubSpot): Client companies, deals and pipeline, contact information, engagement notes - Linear: Engineering tickets, bug tracking, project management - QuickBooks: Invoices, bills, vendor payments, customer billing, financial records - Calendar: Team meetings, client calls, deadlines - Contacts: Internal employee directory and external contact lookup Data often spans multiple systems.

5 The CRM engagement note mentions Andre Washington's status and the communication or cross-system status gap (or similar).
outcome

✓Run #1The CRM note contains 'ISSUE 3: ANDRE WASHINGTON RELOCATION — Status & Communication Gap' detailing the NDA bottleneck, Slack thread findings, and the client-in-the-dark communication failure.

✓Run #5The CRM note mentions Andre Washington's current move (Columbus, GA → Seattle), explains the NDA bottleneck as the primary reason for delay, and notes the cross-system discrepancy: 'The Linear tickets (issues 325-327) for Andre's Atlanta move are assigned to Suki Patel, but the Seattle move logistics are being handled by Fatimah Al-Rashidi per Slack ops updates.' The slowness is attributed entirely to the NDA bottleneck.

The agent did not identify the correct cross-system discrepancy per the criterion.

This likely contributed to the perception of no progress.'
—Run #5The final response identifies the current move as 'Columbus, GA → Seattle' and mentions this is separate from a prior move, and the CRM note mentions 'Detroit → Atlanta (first move completed)'. However, neither the final response nor the CRM note explicitly states that the Columbus→Seattle move has no Airtable or Linear tracking record despite being active in CRM/QuickBooks. The cross-system tracking gap as the cause of 'no updates' is not identified — the NDA bottleneck is given as the primary reason instead.
✗Run #6The final response identifies Columbus→Seattle as Andre's current move and mentions the NDA as a bottleneck, but does NOT identify Detroit→Atlanta as a distinct completed move, and does not explicitly note the cross-system discrepancy (no Airtable/Linear tracking for the current Columbus→Seattle move). The NDA bottleneck is cited as the root cause, not the tracking system gap.
